steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the subject of "_____ will begin to combust." The antecedent is "the substance" (coconut oil) — a singular noun introduced two sentences earlier. The pronoun must be singular and function as a subject.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'they': plural subject pronoun. "The substance" is singular. Number agreement fails. ✗
  • Choice B — 'it': singular subject pronoun. Matches singular "the substance" in both number and grammatical function. Substituting: "the substance will begin to combust" — meaning holds perfectly. ✓
  • Choice C — 'those': plural demonstrative pronoun. Does not agree with singular "the substance." ✗
  • Choice D — 'that': singular demonstrative, but "that" points to something more remote or introduces a relative clause — it is not the standard pronoun used to refer back to a previously named substance as a simple subject. "It" is the correct pronoun for a previously introduced singular thing. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
